description: Synthetic NEW-SYLLABUS ('Curiosity' 2024 NCERT style) mini-chapter authored to MIRROR the real prod format that broke the Wave-1 pilot re-run (grade 6 science ch2), WITHOUT copying any corpus text — all sentences and terms are invented. Two structural signatures are reproduced: (1) a boxed 'Keywords' header separated from its ~20-term list by an intervening prose sidebar ('Did you know?' + a titled note), with terms rendered as newline-separated 1-2 word TitleCase entries (the old 600-char window truncated inside the sidebar and counted ~3 stray fragments); (2) named-but-unnumbered TitleCase sub-section headings ('Natural Materials' / 'Synthetic Materials') that sit below a numbered main heading, alongside two conventional N.M.K numbered subtopics under a different section (the decimal-only regex under-counted these). Authored ground truth in `truth`.

chunks.chunk_text:
3.1 Kinds Of Materials
Everything around us is made of some material.
Natural Materials
Wood, cotton and stone are obtained from nature.
Synthetic Materials
Plastic and nylon are made by people in factories.

3.2 Grouping Materials
Materials can be sorted into groups.
3.2.1 Grouping By Appearance
Some materials are shiny while others are dull.
3.2.2 Grouping By Hardness
Some materials are hard and some are soft.

3.3 Materials In Daily Life
We use many materials every day.
Spoons, plates and clothes are examples of useful things.
Keywords
Did you
know?
Useful Facts About Materials: A Short Note
Many materials can be recycled and used again. Recycling
saves resources and reduces waste in our surroundings.
This simple habit protects the environment for everyone.
Material
Substance

Synthetic material
